title: celtis pumila var. georgiana
intro: Celtis pumila var. georgiana, also known as the Georgia hackberry or dwarf hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Celtis pumila var. georgiana are said to include wound healing, reduction of fever, and relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
